definition: The latest time by which something must be finished.
To use something you already have to get a better result.
To make a process simpler and more efficient.
The time or capacity a person has available for a task.
To pass a problem to someone with more authority.
To give part of your work or authority to someone else.
A specific result or item that must be produced.
A person or group affected by or interested in a project.
A point that slows down the progress of a whole process.
Able to grow or expand without losing efficiency.
A standard used to compare and measure performance.
Specific enough that you can act on it right away.
Agreement between people on goals or direction.
The amount of work completed in a given time.
A significant point marking progress toward a goal.
A plan showing the steps to reach a future goal.
To repeat a process, improving it each time.
To change strategy or direction significantly.
To bring a new employee into a company or team.
To formally end an employee's time at a company.
Following rules, laws, or company policy.
To make a problem or risk less severe.
To decide what is most important and do it first.
To hire an outside company to do certain work.
The ongoing costs of running a business.
No longer needed because it duplicates something else.
The time it takes to complete and return a task.
To make practical use of something.
Capable of working successfully in practice.

Extra benefit created when things work together.

To return to a topic or task later.
Acting in advance to deal with a problem.
A cycle where results are used to improve the next step.
